_comment: Trio trust-model config (single source of truth). Read by bin/lib/trio_policy.sh (shell) and bin/lib/trio_adjudicate.py (python). A re-weight is a one-line edit here. Spec: docs/TRIO_TRUST_MODEL_2026-06-03.md.
_domain_comment: DOMAIN-AWARE policy (Kevin-locked). The top-level weights/sensitive_min_weight stay the flat GENERAL default so every existing reader works unchanged. The domains{} map adds per-domain tables (weights + fleet ratio + per-engine role). Pick a domain by classifying the subject (CSS/HTML/templates/visual => ui; else general) or an explicit --domain ui. One-line-editable: each engine's weight/ratio/role is a single JSON line.
_guard_comment: T0 guard rail (WEIGHTS_PLAN_REVIEW_2026-06-12): this file is tier3 + a gate file. AUTHORITY IS DECLARED, NOT DERIVED: sensitive_authors lists who may first-pass tier3 work and adjudicator names the deciding family, so weight movement alone can never grant or revoke either seat. Both readers clamp: non-adjudicator weights stay strictly below the adjudicator's; declared sensitive_authors stay floored at sensitive_min_weight.

general._about: GENERAL/code domain. Codex = many-agent workhorse author (fast mode + priority tier); Gemini = READ-ONLY checkpoint auditor only; Opus = adjudicator/merger + author.

roles.codex: many-agent workhorse author (fast mode + priority tier)

ui._about: UI/design domain. Gemini = first-class design author AND reviewer (leveraged OVER Codex); Codex = code-correctness (markup/logic/a11y); Opus = adjudicator/merger.

roles.gemini: first-class design author AND reviewer (leveraged over Codex)
